content.problemStatement: Write a program for the coding challenge "Odd-Even Subsequence". Refer to LeetCode description: https://codeforces.com/contest/1370/problem/D
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Then for each test case: The first line contains an integer N, the length of the sequence. The second line contains N space-separated integers, the sequence.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the maximum length of the odd-even subsequence.

hidden.label: tricky case / empty-like / negative if allowed

solution.approach: Use dynamic programming to track the maximum length of the odd-even subsequence ending at each position.

code.Python3:
for _ in range(int(input())):
    N = int(input())
    arr = list(map(int, input().split()))
    dp = [[0, 0] for _ in range(N)]
    dp[0][0] = 1 if arr[0] % 2 == 0 else 0
    dp[0][1] = 1 if arr[0] % 2 == 1 else 0
    for i in range(1, N):
        if arr[i] % 2 == 0:
            dp[i][0] = dp[i-1][1] + 1
            dp[i][1] = dp[i-1][1]
        else:
            dp[i][0] = dp[i-1][0]
            dp[i][1] = dp[i-1][0] + 1
    print(max(dp[N-1][0], dp[N-1][1]))
